mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary shift that lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months with declining FEDFUNDS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand that reduces household income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and payroll employment indicate a coordinated demand-income expansion that amplifies earnings expectations beyond either signal in isolation, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase jointly, or the joint signal Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals that restrictive policy is cooling the labor market, triggering recession fears and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 3% and rising over prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations where UNRATE rises while FEDFUNDS is above 3% and rising, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals a contraction in manufacturing and mining activity that reduces corporate earnings expectations and weighs on cyclical equities, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in retail sales signals robust consumer demand that supports revenue growth expectations and drives positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns as market participants extrapolate spending strength into earnings.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) during months with rising RSAFS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5% signals upside inflation surprises that raise real discount rates and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates a broad-based real economy expansion where manufacturing and construction activity mutually reinforce earnings growth, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when both INDPRO and HOUST increase jointly, or the joint signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.
